semanticCore: Testing, examining, trial, refining leather, or whipping
quranicRelevance: Hearts tested for piety (Imtahana - 'imtahana Allahu quloobahum'), and testing migrating women ('fam-tahinoohunna').

keyDerivations.meaning: To test, examine, try, inspect, verify sincerity, or probe

figurativeOrRareUsages: يَا أَيُّهَا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا إِذَا جَاءَكُمُ الْمُؤْمِنَاتُ مُهَاجِرَاتٍ فَامْتَحِنُوهُنَّ (60:10): O you who believe! When the believing women come to you as emigrants, examine/test them (to verify their political/religious sincerity).
discardedNoise: Softening leather skins in tanning pits (classical reference).
summaryEn: The root represents examining/testing (Imtihan), detailing how God tests hearts for piety (Ikhlas) and commanding the testing of migrating women's loyalty.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر الاختبار والفرز لمعرفة الحقيقة وباطن الأمر (امتحان)، واصفاً تطهير الله لقلوب المتقين، وأمر اختبار صدق المهاجرات سياسياً وعقائدياً.
rawText: مَحُونَةٌ (not مُحُونَةٌ): see art. حين. مِحْنَةٌ A trial, (S, Msb, K,) or trying affliction: (S:) pl. مِحَنٌ. (Msb.)
